steps.title: Find BC using right triangle ABC
steps.content:
Triangle ABC has a right angle at C. Using the Pythagorean theorem with $AC = 8$ and $AB = \sqrt{89}$:
$$BC^2 + AC^2 = AB^2$$
$$BC^2 + 64 = 89$$
$$BC = 5$$

steps.title: Identify that A, B, D are collinear
steps.content:
From the figure, the line through A and B extends to D. The slope of this line is
$$\text{slope} = \frac{BC}{AC} = \frac{5}{8}$$
All three points A, B, and D lie on the same line with this slope.

steps.title: Find the height DE
steps.content:
Point E is at horizontal distance $AE = AC + CE = 8 + 24 = 32$ from A. Since D lies on the line through A with slope $\frac{5}{8}$, and angle DEC is a right angle (D is directly above E), the height is
$$DE = \frac{5}{8} \times 32 = 20$$

steps.title: Calculate the area of triangle ADE
steps.content:
With base $AE = 32$ and height $DE = 20$:
$$\text{Area} = \frac{1}{2} \times 32 \times 20 = 320\text{ square units}$$
The area of triangle ADE is 320.
